summary: Joel Spolsky shares a brief travelogue from the FogBugz World Tour stop in San Francisco and the Bay Area. He describes a pleasant weekend driving around Marin County, a well-attended Monday demo with about 110 people, and a tour of Pixar's offices in Emeryville. He notes the contrast between Steve Jobs' messy private office and Pixar's impressive public spaces, and mentions that while many employees get private offices, space constraints mean some work in cubes.
key_points: The FogBugz World Tour demo in San Francisco drew approximately 110 attendees
Pixar's offices feature impressive public spaces but face space constraints, with a mix of private offices and cubicles
Steve Jobs' office was notably messy, contrasting with his public image of minimalist Zen aesthetics
Joel was demoing the Developer Ship Dates feature in FogBugz 6.0
money_quotes: Steve Jobs' office was, uh, kinda messy. Not Zen at all.
The public spaces are really incredible; a lot of people get private offices, but they're kind of short on room so there are a lot of cubes, too.
key_insight: A lightweight travelogue from the FogBugz product tour, notable mainly for the amusing observation that Steve Jobs' private office didn't match his minimalist public persona.
